name: Aider

form_factor.comment: CLI terminal app. Per-form-factor links: CLI: https://aider.chat/docs/install.html.

released_in.comment: First public release v0.5.0 on June 7, 2023.

latest_major_update.comment: Latest tagged release v0.86.0 (Aug 9, 2025). Main branch is actively developed beyond that.

rules.comment: Supports CONVENTIONS.md loaded via --read or .aider.conf.yml. No equivalent of a persistent auto-loaded CLAUDE.md file. Location: `.aider.conf.yml`, `.aider.model.settings.yml`, `.aider.model.metadata.json`, and repo convention files; no AGENTS.md standard.

skills.comment: Has /save and /load commands to persist/replay sequences of slash commands. Not a full reusable skill system like Cursor skills. Location: no first-class skill folder; use repo files/prompts/conventions.

hooks.comment: Only git pre-commit hook bypass via --git-commit-verify. No lifecycle hooks (session-start, session-end, etc.).

mcp_servers.comment: No MCP (Model Context Protocol) support detected in documentation, codebase, or feature listings. Location: no MCP config for Aider.

custom_commands.comment: 40+ built-in slash commands but no mechanism for users to define custom slash commands. Location: no custom command folder; uses CLI flags/config.

subagents.comment: Architect/Editor mode uses two models but no general subagent delegation system. No spawning of independent worker agents.

model_selection.comment: Full model selection via --model, --weak-model, --editor-model. In-chat /model command. Model aliases supported.

approval_mode.comment: Has --yes-always to auto-accept all confirmations. No granular approval mode like Cursor's always/accept-tool/plan-then-act.

sandbox_mode.comment: No filesystem or network sandbox. Has --dry-run flag that prevents file modification. Aider edits files directly in the working git repo.

resume.comment: No --resume flag. Has --restore-chat-history which restores previous chat messages but does not resume session state.

continue.comment: No --continue flag for auto-resuming the latest session. --restore-chat-history restores message history but is manual.

non_interactive.comment: Full non-interactive mode via --message/-m (send instruction, edit files, exit). Also --apply, --commit, --lint, --test.

output_format.comment: No structured machine-readable output format (JSON, etc.). Has --no-pretty for plain text, --verbose for debug info.

statusline.comment: No configurable status line. Has extensive color customization but no status bar/line concept.

telemetry.comment: Opt-in anonymous analytics via PostHog. Permanently disable with --analytics-disable. Never collects code/chat/keys.

custom_model_provider.comment: Connects to any LLM via litellm. Supports OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, DeepSeek, Ollama, OpenRouter, any OpenAI-compatible endpoint, and more.

notes: Aider is a mature open-source CLI coding agent (45k+ stars) since June 2023. Deep git integration, repo mapping for codebase context, extensive LLM provider support through litellm. No published embed SDK (CLI/scripting only). Notable gaps vs newer agents: no MCP, no user-defined slash commands, no sandbox, no subagent delegation, no resume/continue flags, no structured output. Apache-2.0 licensed.

pricing.comment: Free and open source under Apache 2.0. Users pay only for LLM API usage through their own API keys.

hosted_agent.comment: Runs entirely locally via pip. No hosted agent platform.

transcripts.comment: Location: optional chat history/transcript files are configured with Aider history settings such as `.aider.chat.history.md`.

name: Amp

form_factor.comment: CLI TUI + IDE extensions (VS Code, JetBrains, Neovim, Zed) + @ampcode/sdk (TypeScript) and amp-sdk (Python). Per-form-factor links: CLI: https://ampcode.com/manual#getting-started; Extension: https://ampcode.com/manual/vscode; SDK: https://ampcode.com/manual/sdk.

released_in.comment: Publicly launched May 15, 2025 (no waitlist). Originally built inside Sourcegraph, spun off as Amp Frontier Corporation.

latest_major_update.comment: May 6, 2026 — 'Amp, Rebuilt' (codename Neo). Complete CLI rewrite with new architecture: remote-controllable, plugin-powered, auto-compaction.

rules.comment: Full support via AGENTS.md files (plural). Scanned from cwd, parent dirs, subtrees, $HOME/.config/amp/, /etc/ampcode/. Supports @-mentions, globs, YAML frontmatter. Location: no public disk folder/file location found in docs reviewed.

skills.comment: Full support. Skills are directories with SKILL.md + YAML frontmatter. Locations: .agents/skills/ (project), ~/.config/agents/skills/ (user). Can bundle MCP servers via mcp.json. Location: no public disk folder/file location found in docs reviewed.

hooks.comment: Full support via Plugin API (TypeScript). Events: session.start, agent.start, tool.call, tool.result, agent.end. Plugins can respond with allow/reject/modify/synthesize actions.

mcp_servers.comment: Full support. Add via CLI (amp mcp add) or config. Local (command/args/env) and remote (url/headers) servers. OAuth auto-flow. Skill bundling for lazy loading. Location: no public disk folder/file location found in docs reviewed.

custom_commands.comment: Removed January 29, 2026. Replaced by skills and command palette (Ctrl+O) instead of slash commands. Location: not applicable; feature not supported.

subagents.comment: Full support. Spawned via Task tool. Built-in specialized subagents: Oracle (GPT-5.4), Librarian (code search), Painter (image gen), Review, Search.

model_selection.comment: Pre-set modes (smart=Claude Opus 4.7, deep=GPT-5.5 reasoning, rush=GPT-5.5 no-reasoning, free=mixed). You choose the mode, not the exact model.

approval_mode.comment: Default: no approval (dangerously-allow-all is now default). Custom approval policies possible via Plugin API (tool.call event).

sandbox_mode.comment: No built-in sandbox. Documentation suggests external isolated dev environments for untrusted code.

resume.comment: Full support. 'amp threads continue [thread id]' documented. Thread referencing (@T-xxx or URL) across sessions.

continue.comment: Full support. /continue command in CLI to pick old threads. Threads persist at ampcode.com/threads.

non_interactive.comment: Full support. amp --execute / amp -x for headless mode. Set AMP_API_KEY env var for CI/CD. Auto-detects piped stdout.

output_format.comment: Full support. --stream-json flag with --execute for Claude Code-compatible NDJSON streaming output. Multiple schema types.

statusline.comment: Not supported. Custom themes removed in the May 6, 2026 rebuild. No statusline customization.

telemetry.comment: No explicit telemetry settings found in documentation. No on/off telemetry toggle mentioned in manual or settings.

custom_model_provider.comment: No BYOM for the main agent (picks models per mode from providers). MCP servers can connect to external tools, and Plugin API allows custom tool registration.

notes: Amp is opinionated and aggressively removes features it considers obsolete (custom commands, slash commands, themes). Focuses on frontier models. Plugin API provides extensive extensibility for those who need it. Closed-source product; some auxiliary tools open source on GitHub.

pricing.comment: Usage-based: pay-as-you-go with zero markup on API costs for individuals. Minimum $5 credit purchase. Free mode (ad-supported, no longer shows ads). Enterprise: 50% markup + $1,000 one-time fee.

hosted_agent.comment: CLI runs locally. Threads shared via web but no managed agent platform.

transcripts.comment: Location: transcript/session disk storage was not found in public docs reviewed.

name: Antigravity

form_factor.comment: Google Antigravity IDE plus standalone agy CLI (plugin layout, rules, skills, hooks, MCP). Per-form-factor links: CLI: https://antigravity.google/docs/cli-getting-started; IDE: https://antigravity.google/download.

released_in.comment: IDE public release in 2025; standalone agy CLI documented May 2026.

latest_major_update.comment: CLI plugin system and IDE agent surfaces updated together in current docs.

rules.comment: Full plugin rules on CLI; IDE has persistent agent context with overlapping rules support. Location: Antigravity plugin rules live in plugin folders; workspace/global IDE rule storage is managed by Antigravity.

skills.comment: Plugin skills on CLI; IDE local data includes skills with less public doc detail. Location: plugin skill folders containing `SKILL.md`.

hooks.comment: Plugin hooks documented on CLI.

mcp_servers.comment: CLI plugin mcp_config.json; IDE also ships MCP configuration. Location: plugin `mcp_config.json` files and IDE/CLI MCP configuration.

custom_commands.comment: CLI via plugin capabilities; IDE via global workflows rather than classic slash-command folders. Location: command-like behavior is via plugins/workflows rather than a documented standalone command folder.

model_selection.comment: IDE model UI exists; agy --help does not expose a model flag as of this dataset.

approval_mode.comment: CLI supports --dangerously-skip-permissions; IDE approval behavior not fully documented.

sandbox_mode.comment: CLI supports --sandbox for terminal restrictions.

resume.comment: CLI --continue/--conversation; IDE stores conversation/brain state.

continue.comment: CLI --continue for latest conversation; IDE continues from existing history.

non_interactive.comment: CLI supports --print / --prompt; IDE is interactive-first.

custom_model_provider.comment: No arbitrary provider configuration found in public CLI help/docs.

notes: Google Antigravity spans an agentic IDE and the agy CLI. Distinct from deprecated Gemini CLI. No published programmatic SDK.

pricing.value: Free (generous weekly rate limits), Google AI Pro/Ultra (undisclosed), Organization (consumption-based via Google Cloud)
pricing.comment: Free tier includes Gemini 3.5 Flash, 3.1 Pro, 3 Flash, Claude Sonnet & Opus 4.6, gpt-oss-120b. Pro/Ultra tiers offer more generous rate limits.

hosted_agent.comment: Local IDE and CLI surfaces.

transcripts.comment: Location: Antigravity brain/trajectory summaries are under `~/.gemini/antigravity/brain/<uuid>/*.md`; index data is in `~/Library/Application Support/Antigravity/User/globalStorage/state.vscdb`.

form_factor.comment: CLI, IDE integration, VS Code extension, SDK. Per-form-factor links: CLI: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/quickstart; Extension: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/ide-integrations; SDK: https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/claude-code/sdk; Web: https://claude.ai/code; Mac App: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/desktop-quickstart.

released_in.comment: Claude Code was introduced as a limited research preview.

latest_major_update.comment: Current docs cover CLI, SDK, hooks, skills, and settings.

rules.comment: Uses CLAUDE.md memory files. Location: `CLAUDE.md` in repo tree, plus `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` for user memory.

skills.comment: Supports skills. Location: `.claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md` or user-level `~/.claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md`.

hooks.comment: Supports lifecycle hooks.

mcp_servers.comment: Supports MCP servers. Location: project `.mcp.json` or Claude Code MCP config via `claude mcp`.

custom_commands.comment: Supports slash commands. Location: `.claude/commands/*.md` or `~/.claude/commands/*.md`.

subagents.comment: Supports custom subagents.

model_selection.comment: Model can be configured.

approval_mode.comment: Permission mode is configurable.

sandbox_mode.comment: Permissions and allowed tools provide sandbox-like controls.

resume.comment: Supports --resume.

continue.comment: Supports --continue for the most recent conversation.

non_interactive.comment: Supports --print.

output_format.comment: Supports output format controls.

statusline.comment: Supports custom status line.

telemetry.comment: Settings include telemetry and privacy controls.

custom_model_provider.comment: Supports Bedrock/Vertex and related integrations, not arbitrary providers in the same way as OpenAI-compatible CLIs.

pricing.value: Included in Pro $20/mo, Max 5x $100/mo, Max 20x $200/mo, Team $25-125/seat/mo, Enterprise $20/seat + API rates
pricing.comment: Claude Code is included in all subscription plans (Pro, Max, Team, Enterprise). Also available via API at standard token rates.

hosted_agent.comment: CLI runs locally; managed/cloud-based session execution is not directly exposed as a distinct platform.

transcripts.comment: Location: Claude Code CLI transcripts are JSONL under `~/.claude/projects/<proj-path-dashes>/<sessionId>.jsonl`.

name: Cline

form_factor.comment: VS Code extension, JetBrains plugin, CLI, and @cline/sdk for embedding the agent runtime in apps and CI. Per-form-factor links: Extension: https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=saoudrizwan.claude-dev; CLI: https://docs.cline.bot/cli/overview; SDK: https://docs.cline.bot/sdk/overview.

released_in.comment: Initial release was v1.0.4 on July 28, 2024 (originally named 'Claude Dev' by Saoud Rizwan). 8M+ installs by May 2026.

latest_major_update.comment: CLI at v3.0.10 (May 22, 2026), VS Code extension at v3.84.0 (May 19, 2026). Rapid release cadence.

rules.comment: Rich rule system: .clinerules/ directory, global ~/Documents/Cline/Rules/, conditional rules with YAML frontmatter globs, auto-detection of .cursorrules, .windsurfrules, AGENTS.md. Location: no public disk folder/file location found in docs reviewed.

skills.comment: Full skill system: SKILL.md files in .cline/skills/ or ~/.cline/skills/, YAML frontmatter, progressive loading, slash command triggering, skill toggles. Location: no public disk folder/file location found in docs reviewed.

hooks.comment: Lifecycle hooks via SDK plugin system: 18 hook stages (input, runtime_event, session_start, turn_start, tool_call_before/after, etc.). Also ~/.cline/hooks/ directory.

mcp_servers.comment: Full MCP support with STDIO and HTTP/SSE transports, MCP Marketplace for one-click install, cline mcp CLI wizard. Location: no public disk folder/file location found in docs reviewed.

custom_commands.comment: Built-in slash commands plus skills auto-register as slash commands. SDK plugins can also register custom commands. Location: no public disk folder/file location found in docs reviewed.

subagents.comment: Full subagent system: use_subagents tool spawns parallel read-only research agents with separate context/token budgets.

model_selection.comment: 30+ documented providers: Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, AWS Bedrock, Azure, GCP Vertex, OpenRouter (200+ models), Ollama, LM Studio, and any OpenAI-compatible endpoint.

approval_mode.comment: Granular auto-approve per category: Read, Edit, Execute, Browser, MCP. YOLO mode for everything. --auto-approve flag.

sandbox_mode.comment: Sandbox mode via CLINE_SANDBOX env var and CLINE_SANDBOX_DATA_DIR for isolated session storage. No containerization.

resume.comment: Full session resume via --id flag. cline history lists all sessions. Checkpoints enable rollback.

continue.comment: --continue flag exists but is tied to --worktree. No standalone 'continue latest' flag. Pipe-chaining supported.

non_interactive.comment: Full headless mode. Auto-triggered with --json flag, piped stdin, or redirected stdout. Works in CI/CD.

output_format.comment: Full JSON output via --json flag. Newline-delimited JSON objects with type, text, ts, say/ask subtype, reasoning, partial.

statusline.comment: No customizable status line found in documentation or CLI reference.

telemetry.comment: Anonymous telemetry with user toggle. Never captures code/file contents. Enterprise: OpenTelemetry integration (OTLP export to Datadog/Grafana/Splunk).

custom_model_provider.comment: Any OpenAI-compatible API endpoint supported. 30+ documented providers. BYOK model.

notes: Cline has the most comprehensive feature set of any coding agent in this matrix — 17/20 fields at full support. Open source (Apache 2.0), originally 'Claude Dev'. Started as VS Code extension, expanded to CLI and SDK. 8M+ installs.

pricing.comment: Open source: free for individual developers. Pay only for AI inference. Enterprise: custom pricing with SSO/OIDC/SCIM, RBAC.

hosted_agent.comment: Runs locally as VS Code extension or CLI.

transcripts.comment: Location: transcript/session disk storage was not found in public docs reviewed.

form_factor.comment: CLI + Open VSX extension + hosted on platform.openai.com. TypeScript (@openai/codex-sdk) and experimental Python SDKs for programmatic control. Per-form-factor links: CLI: https://developers.openai.com/codex/cli/; Extension: https://developers.openai.com/codex/ide/; SDK: https://developers.openai.com/codex/sdk/; Web: https://chatgpt.com/codex; Mac App: https://developers.openai.com/codex/app/.

released_in.comment: Open-source CLI public release.

latest_major_update.comment: Captured from current Codex docs/changelog state.

rules.comment: Uses AGENTS.md instructions. Location: `AGENTS.md` in repo tree, plus user/project Codex config for defaults.

skills.comment: Supports SKILL.md-based skills. Location: `.codex/skills/<name>/SKILL.md`, `~/.codex/skills/<name>/SKILL.md`, or installed plugin skill folders.

hooks.comment: Supports Codex hooks.

mcp_servers.comment: Supports MCP servers. Location: `~/.codex/config.toml` under MCP/server configuration.

custom_commands.comment: CLI supports slash-like workflows through prompts and tooling, but not a first-class command folder in this matrix. Location: no separate command folder documented; command-like workflows are provided by prompts, skills, plugins, and CLI slash commands.

subagents.comment: Codex supports sub-agent/delegation workflows in agent contexts.

model_selection.comment: CLI accepts model configuration/flags.

approval_mode.comment: Approval policy can be configured.

sandbox_mode.comment: Filesystem sandbox modes can be configured.

resume.comment: Supports resume.

continue.comment: Supports continuing a previous session through resume/latest-session workflows.

non_interactive.comment: Supports exec/headless use.

output_format.comment: CLI supports structured output modes.

statusline.comment: Supports statusline configuration in local CLI settings.

telemetry.comment: Telemetry settings are documented in the CLI repository.

custom_model_provider.comment: Supports custom model providers in config.

pricing.value: Free (limited), Plus $20/mo, Pro $100-200/mo, Business pay-as-you-go, API usage-based
pricing.comment: Free tier has limited Codex access. Plus ($20/mo) includes 15-350 local messages/5h. Pro $100/mo at 5x limits ($200/mo at 20x). Business at pay-as-you-go token credits. API key at per-token pricing.

hosted_agent.comment: Can run locally or on platform.openai.com managed infrastructure.

transcripts.comment: Location: Codex session event streams are JSONL under `~/.codex/sessions/YYYY/MM/DD/rollout-<iso>-<id>.jsonl`.

name: Cohere North

form_factor.comment: Enterprise AI agent studio/workspace with UI and API-based workflows. Not a repo-native coding agent. Per-form-factor links: Web: https://cohere.com/north; SDK: https://cohere.com/north/agent-studio.

released_in.comment: North early access announcement.

latest_major_update.comment: Command A+ model update powers agentic workflows and coding benchmarks, while North remains the enterprise agent platform.

rules.comment: North supports enterprise guardrails, but not repo-local instruction files. Location: hosted/web/API-managed; no local disk folder documented.

skills.comment: No-code custom agents and reusable workflows, not code-agent SKILL.md folders. Location: hosted/web/API-managed; no local disk folder documented.

hooks.comment: Multi-step workflow automations are a core Agent Studio feature.

mcp_servers.comment: Agent Studio explicitly mentions MCP integration. Location: hosted/web/API-managed; no local disk folder documented.

custom_commands.comment: No coding-agent slash-command surface found. Location: hosted/web/API-managed; no local disk folder documented.

subagents.comment: Agent-to-agent coordination is listed as an extensibility feature.

model_selection.comment: Cohere model stack is selected through North/Cohere platform, not arbitrary coding-agent CLI flags.

approval_mode.comment: Enterprise guardrails and human-agent collaboration; no per-shell-command approval model.

sandbox_mode.comment: Private deployment options include VPC, on-prem, or Cohere Model Vault.

resume.comment: North has memory-oriented agent workflows, but no coding-session resume command.

continue.comment: Memory supports cross-session workflows in North; not a CLI continue feature.

non_interactive.comment: API-based workflows can automate tasks, but not local repo headless coding.

output_format.comment: Cohere APIs return structured responses; North UI outputs are workflow-specific.

statusline.comment: Enterprise web workspace, no CLI statusline.

telemetry.comment: Audit-ready logs and traceability are documented; telemetry opt-out details are not.

hosted_agent.comment: North is an enterprise agent platform/workspace.

custom_model_provider.comment: Built around Cohere models/platform deployment, not arbitrary model providers.

pricing.comment: North is sold through demo/sales motion.

notes: Included because the request mentioned Cohere. This is an enterprise agent platform with coding-capable Command A+ underneath, not a direct Claude Code/Codex-style coding agent.

transcripts.comment: Location: hosted/web/API-managed session history; no local transcript directory documented.

name: Cursor

form_factor.comment: VS Code-derived AI IDE with standalone CLI (cursor-agent) and @cursor/sdk (TypeScript/Python) for programmatic local or cloud agents. Per-form-factor links: IDE: https://cursor.com/downloads; CLI: https://docs.cursor.com/en/cli/installation; SDK: https://cursor.com/docs/sdk/typescript.

released_in.comment: IDE launched ~2023; CLI surface added later (2025).

latest_major_update.comment: Current docs capture rules, skills, MCP, models, and agent workflows.

rules.comment: Supports project and user rules. Location: `.cursor/rules/*.mdc` in a project; user/global rules are configured in Cursor settings.

skills.comment: Supports skills. Location: Cursor rules/agents features, not a SKILL.md folder standard in this row.

hooks.comment: No lifecycle hooks documented for IDE or CLI.

mcp_servers.comment: Supports MCP servers in both IDE and CLI. Location: `.cursor/mcp.json` for project MCP servers or user-level Cursor MCP settings.

custom_commands.comment: CLI exposes full command controls; IDE has reusable rule/workflow patterns. Location: custom command support is through Cursor features/SDK rather than a repo command folder.

subagents.comment: Agent tasks are supported; first-class named subagents not modeled like Claude Code.

model_selection.comment: Supports model settings in IDE and CLI.

approval_mode.comment: Agent modes and CLI yolo/force controls expose approval controls.

sandbox_mode.comment: CLI exposes sandbox controls; IDE trust and agent controls provide partial sandboxing.

resume.comment: Composer/chat history in IDE and CLI resume/continue workflows support session resumption.

continue.comment: Supports --continue in CLI and conversation history in IDE.

non_interactive.comment: CLI supports print/headless mode; IDE is interactive only.

output_format.comment: CLI has partial structured output support; IDE has none.

statusline.comment: Editor status bar customization inherited from VS Code, not an agent-specific statusline.

telemetry.comment: Cursor documents privacy settings.

hosted_agent.comment: CLI agent can execute in Cursor's cloud runtime.

custom_model_provider.comment: Supports custom API keys/models in IDE and CLI.

notes: Cursor is a VS Code-derived AI IDE with a companion CLI (cursor-agent) for headless/CI use and @cursor/sdk for programmatic local/cloud agents.

pricing.comment: Individual: Pro $20/mo ($20 API usage), Pro+ $60/mo ($70 API usage), Ultra $200/mo ($400 API usage). Teams $40/user/mo.

transcripts.comment: Location: Cursor CLI transcripts live under `~/.cursor/projects/<slug>/agent-transcripts/<agentId>/<agentId>.jsonl`; Cursor IDE composer data is in SQLite `~/Library/Application Support/Cursor/User/globalStorage/state.vscdb`.

name: Devin

form_factor.comment: Web app + VS Code extension + CLI + Slack/Teams + REST API and official devin-sdk (Python). Per-form-factor links: Extension: https://docs.devin.ai/work-with-devin/ide-extensions; CLI: https://docs.devin.ai/work-with-devin/devin-cli; Web: https://app.devin.ai/; SDK: https://docs.devin.ai/api-reference/overview.

released_in.comment: First publicly demoed March 2024. Made Generally Available (GA) on December 10, 2024.

latest_major_update.comment: Devin 2.2 (Feb 24, 2026): 3x faster startup, end-to-end desktop testing, Devin Review Autofix, redesigned UI. Continuous weekly releases since.

rules.comment: AGENTS.md files in repos, Knowledge (persistent org-level instructions with trigger descriptions), Skills & Rules settings. Location: hosted/web/API-managed; no local disk folder documented.

skills.comment: Full Agent Skills standard support. SKILL.md files in .agents/skills/. Supports YAML frontmatter, dynamic $ARGUMENTS and !command blocks. Also Playbooks system. Location: hosted/web/API-managed; no local disk folder documented.

hooks.comment: Automations engine: Slack/GitHub/Linear/schedule/cron/webhook triggers. Actions include start session, message session, triage. Payload filtering and secret auth.

mcp_servers.comment: MCP Marketplace with 40+ pre-built integrations. Supports STDIO, SSE, and HTTP transports. Custom MCP server tools for session/playbook/knowledge management. Location: hosted/web/API-managed; no local disk folder documented.

custom_commands.comment: Built-in slash commands (/plan, /review, /test, /think-hard, /implement) plus org-level custom slash commands. Also @skills:name syntax and macros. Location: hosted/web/API-managed; no local disk folder documented.

subagents.comment: Yes, called 'Managed Devins'. Coordinator spawns child Devin sessions in isolated VMs for parallel work. Auto-triage also spawns sub-devins.

model_selection.comment: Not explicitly user-facing. Devin offers 'Fast Mode' (~2x faster at 4x ACU). No documentation about letting users choose the underlying LLM model.

approval_mode.comment: 'Secure Mode' (disables native deployment, restricts outbound access). ACU caps, RBAC, PR review approval. No granular approval per action.

sandbox_mode.comment: Full isolation. Every session gets a fresh ephemeral snapshot. Enterprise VPC deployment option. VPN configuration supported.

resume.comment: API auto-resumes suspended sessions. CLI /resume alias. PR Resuming feature (Apr 2026). Archive/unarchive with undo.

continue.comment: CLI /handoff transfers to cloud Devin with full context (git branch + conversation history). API session messaging for continuation.

non_interactive.comment: Full headless/API mode. REST API with service user auth. Automations run fully autonomously (CI fixers, scheduled sessions, auto-triage).

output_format.comment: API returns structured JSON. Sessions support structured output schemas. No documented machine-readable mode for the CLI itself.

statusline.comment: No statusline feature found in any documentation. Web app shows session status indicators but no CLI statusline.

telemetry.comment: No user-configurable telemetry setting found in documentation. Enterprise audit logs available. Privacy controls mentioned but no opt-in/opt-out toggle.

custom_model_provider.comment: Cerebras mentioned as enterprise-ready inference provider. No self-serve BYO model option documented.

notes: Devin pioneered the autonomous AI software engineer category. Focuses on enterprise: VM isolation, managed sub-agents, automations. Higher-end pricing reflects the hosted, managed approach. Windsurf acquisition announced April 2026.

pricing.comment: Free (limited), Pro ($20/mo), Max ($200/mo), Teams ($80/mo minimum). Enterprise: custom pricing with VPC, SAML/OIDC. ACU-based consumption model.

hosted_agent.comment: Primarily cloud-hosted at app.devin.ai; Devin CLI bridges to cloud agent.

transcripts.comment: Location: hosted/web/API-managed session history; no local transcript directory documented.

name: Factory Droid

form_factor.comment: Droid CLI plus Factory App, IDE integrations, and Droid Computers. Per-form-factor links: CLI: https://docs.factory.ai/reference/cli-reference; Extension: https://docs.factory.ai/cli/integrations/ide-integrations; Web: https://app.factory.ai/.

released_in.comment: Public docs cover current Droid CLI and Factory platform surfaces.

latest_major_update.comment: Current CLI reference includes plugins, MCP, skills, hooks, worktrees, Droid Exec, and custom droids.

rules.comment: AGENTS.md appears in Factory configuration docs. Location: `AGENTS.md` and `.factory/` project configuration.

skills.comment: Skills can be managed and invoked from the CLI. Location: Factory Skills configuration in project/user Factory config; exact folder varies by scope.

hooks.comment: Lifecycle hooks are first-class slash-command managed features.

mcp_servers.comment: Supports stdio and HTTP MCP servers, registry browsing, env vars, and headers. Location: user `~/.factory/mcp.json` and project MCP configuration.

custom_commands.comment: Custom slash commands are directly managed in the CLI. Location: Factory custom slash-command configuration in project/user Factory config.

subagents.comment: Custom Droids are documented as subagents.

model_selection.comment: Model IDs can be selected with -m/--model and /model.

approval_mode.comment: Tiered autonomy levels control action permissions.

sandbox_mode.comment: Docs include Sandbox Beta and unsafe mode guidance for disposable containers.

resume.comment: Resume by session ID or last modified session.

continue.comment: Exec can continue an existing session; interactive defaults to the last modified session.

non_interactive.comment: droid exec is the headless automation mode.

output_format.comment: Search supports JSON output; API and readiness endpoints expose structured data.

statusline.comment: Custom status line is documented.

telemetry.comment: Analytics/compliance APIs exist, but no user telemetry opt-out setting found.

hosted_agent.comment: Factory App and Droid Computers exist; CLI can register machines and run daemon/server flows.

custom_model_provider.comment: Bring Your Own Key/custom model path is documented.

pricing.value: Factory plans; usage details in Factory pricing

name: GitHub Copilot

form_factor.comment: IDE agent mode (VS Code, JetBrains, Xcode, Neovim, Eclipse) + CLI + hosted cloud agent + @github/copilot-sdk (public preview). Per-form-factor links: IDE: https://docs.github.com/en/copilot/using-github-copilot/getting-code-suggestions-in-your-ide; Extension: https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=GitHub.copilot; CLI: https://docs.github.com/en/copilot/using-github-copilot/using-github-copilot-in-the-command-line; SDK: https://docs.github.com/en/copilot/how-tos/copilot-sdk; Web: https://github.com/features/copilot.

released_in.comment: Announced at GitHub Universe (Oct/Nov 2024) in preview. GA billing launch: June 18, 2025 when premium request billing began.

latest_major_update.comment: Copilot desktop app (technical preview): parallel agent sessions, PR lifecycle, scheduled workflows. Third-party agent support added. Usage-based billing announced.

rules.comment: Multiple layers: .github/copilot-instructions.md (repo-wide), path-specific .github/instructions/**, AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, GEMINI.md, personal instructions, org-level. Location: no public disk folder/file location found in docs reviewed.

skills.comment: Open Agent Skills standard. .github/skills, .claude/skills, .agents/skills. Personal skills in ~/.copilot/skills. gh skill CLI for discovery/install. Location: no public disk folder/file location found in docs reviewed.

hooks.comment: Full lifecycle hook system: sessionStart, sessionEnd, userPromptSubmitted, preToolUse (approve/deny), postToolUse, agentStop, subagentStop, errorOccurred. .github/hooks/*.json.

mcp_servers.comment: Full MCP in cloud agent, CLI, and IDE. Supports local, stdio, http, sse transports. Built-in GitHub MCP server. Enterprise allowlist controls. Location: no public disk folder/file location found in docs reviewed.

custom_commands.comment: Built-in slash commands in IDE (/fix, /tests, /explain, /help) and CLI (/delegate, /pr, /resume). Prompt files (*.prompt.md) for reusable snippets. No user-defined custom slash commands. Location: no public disk folder/file location found in docs reviewed.

subagents.comment: Subagents in VS Code and JetBrains IDE agent mode. Isolated context window. Invocation: automatic, direct, or via #runSubagent. Cannot create sub-subagents.

model_selection.comment: Models from Anthropic, Google, OpenAI, xAI. Auto model selection available. Each model has a premium request multiplier.

approval_mode.comment: Desktop app has Interactive, Plan, and Autopilot modes. preToolUse hook can approve/deny. No universal 'always ask before X' toggle.

sandbox_mode.comment: Cloud agent runs in ephemeral GitHub Actions environment (natural sandbox). CLI has tool permission controls. No explicit sandbox toggle.

resume.comment: CLI: copilot --resume opens session picker, copilot --resume SESSION-ID jumps to specific session. /resume during interactive session.

continue.comment: CLI: copilot --continue resumes most recent session directly. IDE agent sessions persistent. Desktop app sessions in sidebar.

non_interactive.comment: CLI programmatic/scripted usage in GitHub Actions. --non-interactive mode. Copilot SDK for API-based integration.

output_format.comment: Copilot SDK provides programmatic access with structured responses. No documented --json flag for general CLI output.

statusline.comment: No statusline feature documented anywhere in GitHub Copilot's documentation.

telemetry.comment: Enterprise audit logging, agent session monitoring. Data retention: prompts retained 28 days. Users can opt out of training data. No granular telemetry UI.

custom_model_provider.comment: BYOK in CLI: env vars for provider type, base URL, API key. OpenAI-compatible endpoints, Azure OpenAI, Anthropic, local models via Ollama.

notes: GitHub Copilot's agent capabilities span IDE, cloud, and CLI. The cloud agent is unique: runs in ephemeral GitHub Actions environment. Desktop app (preview) adds parallel agent sessions. Ecosystem is rapidly evolving with weekly feature drops.

pricing.comment: Free: $0/mo (50 premium requests). Pro: $10/mo (300 premium requests). Pro+: $39/mo (1500 premium requests). Usage-based billing starting June 2026.

hosted_agent.comment: Copilot Cloud Agent runs autonomously in GitHub Actions-powered ephemeral environment.

transcripts.comment: Location: transcript/session disk storage was not found in public docs reviewed.

continue.comment: Designed for async continuation in hosted tasks; no CLI continue command.

name: Kilo Code

form_factor.comment: VS Code extension, JetBrains plugin, CLI, Cloud Agents. OpenCode fork; programmatic control via @opencode-ai/sdk against the Kilo/OpenCode server. Per-form-factor links: CLI: https://kilo.ai/docs/code-with-ai/platforms/cli; Extension: https://kilo.ai/docs/code-with-ai/platforms/vscode.

released_in.comment: Repository created 2025-03-10. First stable public release effectively Feb 2026 (v1.0.25). v7 rewrite announced May 2026.

latest_major_update.comment: Latest release: v7.2.52 (2026-05-11). v7.x series started with v7.0.26 (2026-02-23). 98+ releases total.

rules.comment: Full support: Project Rules (via instructions key in kilo.jsonc), Global Rules (same in ~/.config/kilo/kilo.jsonc), AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, CONTEXT.md. Location: no public disk folder/file location found in docs reviewed.

skills.comment: Full Agent Skills standard. SKILL.md files with YAML frontmatter. Global ~/.kilo/skills/, project .kilo/skills/. Compat with .claude/skills/, .agents/skills/. Location: no public disk folder/file location found in docs reviewed.

hooks.comment: Full support via Plugin system. Lifecycle hooks: config, event (bus events), tool hooks (execute.before/after), chat hooks, permission hooks, provider hooks. 17+ hook types.

mcp_servers.comment: Full MCP support. Local (STDIO) and remote (HTTP Streamable) transports. OAuth 2.0. Tool permissions via allow/ask/deny. Built-in Playwright MCP. Location: no public disk folder/file location found in docs reviewed.

custom_commands.comment: Full support via Workflows (slash commands). Markdown files in ~/.config/kilo/commands/ or .kilo/commands/. YAML frontmatter with description, agent, model. Location: no public disk folder/file location found in docs reviewed.

subagents.comment: Full subagent support. Custom modes/agents with mode property: primary, subagent, or all. Per-agent prompts, models, permissions, temperature. task tool spawns sub-agents.

model_selection.comment: 500+ models via 15+ providers. Switch mid-session with /models. Per-agent model pinning. CLI flags: -m, --model. Auto model routing.

approval_mode.comment: Three-tier: allow (auto-approve), ask (prompt), deny (block). Glob patterns. --auto flag for autonomous mode. Per-agent permission overrides.

sandbox_mode.comment: No dedicated Docker/sandbox isolation. External_directory permissions control reads/writes outside workspace. Permission system with allow/deny/ask.

resume.comment: Session continuation via --continue/-c flag. -s, --session <id> for specific sessions. --fork to fork existing sessions. /sessions slash command.

continue.comment: --continue/-c flag on kilo, kilo run, and kilo attach commands. /sessions slash command also accepts /continue alias.

non_interactive.comment: Multiple non-interactive modes: kilo run <message> (one-shot), --auto flag (fully autonomous), --json (JSON output), --json-io (bidirectional JSON). kilo serve for headless server.

output_format.comment: --format flag with choices default (TUI) and json (raw JSON events). --json flag implies auto mode. --json-io for bidirectional JSON. Session export/import.

statusline.comment: No customizable statusline feature found. Has /status slash command and /themes for theming, but no persistent status line customization.

telemetry.comment: Enabled by default. Disable via experimental.openTelemetry: false. OpenTelemetry export when OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT is set.

custom_model_provider.comment: BYOK for 15+ providers. Plugin system for entirely custom providers. Local models supported. 500+ models via Kilo Gateway at zero markup.

notes: Kilo Code is a rapidly evolving open-source coding agent (MIT, 19k+ stars). Originally forked from Continue, now independent. Notable for extensive plugin system, 500+ model support through Kilo Gateway, and cross-surface session sync.

pricing.comment: App is free & MIT. Kilo Pass (Starter $19/mo, Pro $49/mo, Expert $199/mo). BYOK (no markup). Free models. Teams: $15/user/mo.

hosted_agent.comment: Cloud Agents feature allows web-based sessions; primary use is local via extension/CLI.

transcripts.comment: Location: transcript/session disk storage was not found in public docs reviewed.

name: Kimi CLI

form_factor.comment: CLI + VS Code + Zed + JetBrains via ACP, plus @moonshot-ai/kimi-agent-sdk (Node/Go/Python). Per-form-factor links: CLI: https://github.com/MoonshotAI/kimi-cli; Extension: https://www.kimi.com/help/kimi-code/vscode-getting-started; SDK: https://github.com/MoonshotAI/kimi-agent-sdk.

released_in.comment: First public release v0.34 on 2025-10-21. Reached v1.0 on 2026-01-27. Latest: v1.44.0 (2026-05-14).

latest_major_update.comment: v1.44.0 (2026-05-14). Very active development: 97 releases in ~7 months. Rapid iteration pace.

rules.comment: Full AGENTS.md support. Hierarchical loading from git root to working dir, including .kimi/AGENTS.md at each level (32 KiB budget). /init command auto-generates AGENTS.md. Location: no public disk folder/file location found in docs reviewed.

skills.comment: Full Agent Skills format (SKILL.md with YAML frontmatter). Four-tier auto-discovery. Cross-compatible with Claude/Codex skill dirs. Flow skills with Mermaid/D2 flowcharts. Location: no public disk folder/file location found in docs reviewed.

hooks.comment: Supported (Beta). 13 lifecycle events: PreToolUse, PostToolUse, SessionStart, SessionEnd, SubagentStart/Stop, PreCompact, PostCompact, Notification. Shell commands via stdin.

mcp_servers.comment: Full MCP support. Transport: streamable HTTP, stdio, OAuth. kimi mcp add/list/test/remove/auth commands. Config in ~/.kimi/mcp.json. Location: no public disk folder/file location found in docs reviewed.

custom_commands.comment: Custom plugins (Beta) via plugin.json with executable scripts. Slash commands (/skill:, /flow:) serve as custom invocation. No arbitrary custom aliases in config. Location: no public disk folder/file location found in docs reviewed.

subagents.comment: Full subagent support. Built-in types: coder, explore, plan. YAML agent files with inheritance. Agent tool with subagent_type, model, resume, run_in_background params.

model_selection.comment: Full model selection. Provider types: kimi, openai_legacy, openai_responses, anthropic, gemini, vertexai. /model interactive picker. CLI flags: --model, --thinking.

approval_mode.comment: Full system: Normal (approval with diff preview), YOLO mode (--yolo, auto-approves all), AFK mode (--afk, auto-approves and dismisses questions). Status bar badges.

sandbox_mode.comment: No dedicated sandbox/mode. Has workspace scope boundaries, file operation approval, sensitive file protection (.env, SSH keys). No containerized isolation.

resume.comment: Full session resume. --session/--resume/-S/-r with optional session ID. Interactive picker. Auto-restores approval decisions, plan mode, subagent instances.

continue.comment: kimi --continue/-C resumes most recent session in current working directory. /new for fresh session. /sessions lists and switches between sessions.

non_interactive.comment: --print flag for print mode: auto-exit after prompt, implicit auto-approval (AFK behavior), text stdout. --quiet shorthand. Exit codes: 0=success, 1=error, 75=retryable.

output_format.comment: --input-format: text or stream-json (JSONL). --output-format: text or stream-json (JSONL). --final-message-only flag. --quiet shorthand. Unified Message format for JSON I/O.

statusline.comment: Full status bar in shell mode: context usage %, mode indicators, YOLO/AFK badges, plan mode badge, background task counts, provider display name.

telemetry.comment: No explicit telemetry documentation found. Has automatic update checks (disable via KIMI_CLI_NO_AUTO_UPDATE), but no stated telemetry/analytics collection policy.

custom_model_provider.comment: Full custom provider support. providers table in config.toml with type, base_url, api_key, custom_headers. Six provider types including OpenAI-compatible, Anthropic, Gemini.

notes: Kimi CLI by Moonshot AI (Beijing) is an Apache-2.0 open-source coding agent with rapid development pace (97 releases in 7 months). Strong AGENTS.md, skills, MCP, subagent, and YOLO/AFK approval mode support. Notable for status bar with badges and comprehensive JSON streaming output.

pricing.comment: Kimi Code platform via membership subscription (credits-based, 7-day refresh cycle) or pay-as-you-go via api.moonshot.cn. Also supports own API key from OpenAI/Anthropic/Gemini.

hosted_agent.comment: Local CLI tool backed by Moonshot API.

transcripts.comment: Location: transcript/session disk storage was not found in public docs reviewed.

hooks.comment: Smart hooks are documented as core CLI features.

mcp_servers.comment: Native MCP support in IDE and CLI. Location: `.kiro/mcp.json` / Kiro MCP configuration.

custom_commands.comment: CLI has slash commands and prompt/custom-agent workflows. Location: CLI slash commands are built in; custom agents/prompts are managed in Kiro custom-agent configuration.

subagents.comment: Built-in subagents for context gathering and parallel general-purpose tasks.

model_selection.comment: CLI /model displays and selects available models.

approval_mode.comment: Custom agents can use predefined tool permissions and approved plans.

sandbox_mode.comment: Permissioning is documented; explicit local filesystem/network sandbox modes are not clear.

continue.comment: kiro chat preserves project conversation history for continuing work.

non_interactive.comment: Headless --print examples are documented for CI/CD automation.

output_format.comment: Can export conversation state to JSON; no broad JSON stream mode found.

statusline.comment: No user-configurable statusline docs found.

telemetry.comment: No telemetry control found in the docs reviewed.

hosted_agent.comment: Kiro Web exists, but the public CLI docs emphasize local terminal operation.

custom_model_provider.comment: Docs describe Kiro's model set/Auto agent, not arbitrary model providers.

pricing.comment: CLI and IDE share Kiro subscription pricing.

notes: Spec/steering-first agentic IDE plus CLI. Particularly strong on steering, hooks, custom agents, ACP-compatible IDEs, and CI automation.

transcripts.comment: Location: Kiro CLI uses directory-based conversation persistence; exact transcript directory is not specified in the public docs reviewed.

form_factor.comment: Terminal UI and CLI plus @opencode-ai/sdk for programmatic server/session control. Per-form-factor links: CLI: https://opencode.ai/docs/; Extension: https://opencode.ai/docs/ide/; SDK: https://opencode.ai/docs/sdk/.

released_in.comment: Exact public launch month not confirmed in public docs captured for this dataset.

latest_major_update.comment: Current docs capture config, agents, MCP, and providers.

rules.comment: Supports instruction/config files. Location: `AGENTS.md` and opencode config files in the repo/user config scope.

skills.comment: Supports skills. Location: opencode Agent Skills are stored as skill folders with `SKILL.md` according to the configured scope.

hooks.comment: Config supports commands/hooks workflows.

mcp_servers.comment: Supports MCP servers. Location: `opencode.json` / opencode config MCP server entries.

custom_commands.comment: Supports commands. Location: opencode custom commands are configured under opencode command/config files, not shell aliases.

subagents.comment: Supports custom agents.

model_selection.comment: Supports provider/model selection.

approval_mode.comment: Permission controls are configurable.

sandbox_mode.comment: Permission model controls tool access.

resume.comment: Supports session options.

continue.comment: Supports --continue and explicit session selection.

non_interactive.comment: Supports run mode.

output_format.comment: CLI supports format options.

telemetry.comment: Config includes telemetry-related settings.

custom_model_provider.comment: Supports many providers and custom provider configuration.

pricing.value: Free (MIT, BYOK), Go $10/mo (first month $5), Zen pay-per-token (min $20), Enterprise per-seat
pricing.comment: Free tier: bring your own API key + limited free models. Go ($10/mo): curated open-source models with usage caps. Zen: pay-per-token access to all models (min $20 top-up). Enterprise: per-seat custom pricing.

hosted_agent.comment: Runs entirely locally.

transcripts.comment: Location: OpenCode stores sessions/messages in SQLite `~/.local/share/opencode/opencode.db`; legacy storage uses `~/.local/share/opencode/storage/session|message|part/`.

name: Qwen Code

form_factor.comment: CLI + VS Code + Zed + JetBrains via ACP, plus @qwen-code/sdk (TypeScript) and Python SDK. Per-form-factor links: CLI: https://qwenlm.github.io/qwen-code-docs/getting-started/; Extension: https://qwenlm.github.io/qwen-code-docs/en/users/extension/introduction/; SDK: https://qwenlm-qwen-code.mintlify.app/sdk/quick-start.

released_in.comment: GitHub repo created 2025-06-26. Earliest releases (v0.0.5+) shortly after. Currently at v0.16.0 (2026-05-21).

latest_major_update.comment: Latest release v0.16.0 (May 21, 2026). 453 releases, 5,944 commits as of May 2026.

rules.comment: Hierarchical context files (QWEN.md) at project/user levels. Custom system prompts via --system-prompt and --append-system-prompt. Layered config across system/user/project/env/cli. Location: Qwen Code project/user instruction files and extension packages; commonly `AGENTS.md`-style repo context plus Qwen config.

skills.comment: Full Agent Skills system: SKILL.md with YAML frontmatter, model-invoked, personal (~/.qwen/skills/) and project (.qwen/skills/), path gating, extension skills. Location: Qwen Code extensions package skills via `qwen-extension.json`.

hooks.comment: 14 hook events: PreToolUse, PostToolUse, SessionStart, SessionEnd, SubagentStart/Stop, PreCompact, PostCompact, Notification, PermissionRequest. Three executors: command, http, function.

mcp_servers.comment: Full MCP: stdio, SSE, HTTP transports. OAuth 2.0, tool filtering, server trust, global allow/deny lists. CLI management via qwen mcp commands. Location: Qwen Code MCP settings in Qwen config/extension packages.

custom_commands.comment: Full custom commands: Markdown files in ~/.qwen/commands/ (global) and .qwen/commands/ (project). Support {{args}}, !{shell}, @{file} references. Namespace support. Location: Qwen Code extensions can package custom commands via `qwen-extension.json`.

subagents.comment: Full subagent system: named subagents with YAML frontmatter, fork subagents (context-inheriting), per-agent model/permission/tool config, extension subagents.

model_selection.comment: Full model switching via /model. Supports OpenAI-compatible, Anthropic, Gemini protocols. Local models via Ollama/vLLM. Per-model reasoning config.

approval_mode.comment: Four modes: Plan (read-only), Default (manual approval), Auto-Edit (auto file edits, manual shell), YOLO (auto all). Shift+Tab to cycle.

sandbox_mode.comment: Full sandboxing: macOS Seatbelt (sandbox-exec with 6 profiles) and Docker/Podman containers (full isolation, custom images, network proxying, UID/GID mapping).

resume.comment: /resume command in interactive mode. --resume <sessionId> in headless mode. Session data as JSONL under ~/.qwen/projects/.

continue.comment: --continue flag resumes the most recent session for the current project in headless mode.

non_interactive.comment: -p/--prompt for headless. stdin piping, JSON output, resume/continue, persistent retry mode for CI. GitHub Actions integration.

output_format.comment: Three formats: text (default), json (structured array), stream-json (line-delimited, real-time). --include-partial-messages for granular streaming.

statusline.comment: Custom status line via ui.statusLine in settings.json. Shell command receives JSON context. Multi-line support, hot reload, refreshInterval for timer-based updates.

telemetry.comment: OpenTelemetry-based: traces, logs, metrics. Export to local file, OTLP collector, Alibaba Cloud Managed Service. Privacy opt-out via privacy.usageStatisticsEnabled.

custom_model_provider.comment: modelProviders config with openai/anthropic/gemini auth types. Local models via any OpenAI-compatible endpoint (Ollama, vLLM, LM Studio). OpenRouter, Fireworks AI.

notes: Qwen Code by Alibaba (QwenLM) is the most feature-complete open-source coding agent in this matrix — 20/20 fields at full (tied with Cline). Standout features: macOS Seatbelt + Docker sandbox, OpenTelemetry telemetry, custom status line, three output formats. Apache-2.0, 24.6k stars.

pricing.comment: Tool is free and open-source (Apache 2.0). Model costs: BYO API key (provider pricing), Alibaba Cloud Coding Plan (fixed monthly), or local models (free).

hosted_agent.comment: Can use Alibaba Cloud Coding Plan for hosted model access; agent runs locally.

transcripts.comment: Location: transcript/session disk storage was not found in public docs reviewed.

name: Replit Agent

form_factor.comment: Hosted Replit workspace agent focused on building, testing, and deploying apps. Per-form-factor links: Web: https://replit.com/ai.

released_in.comment: Replit Agent launched publicly in 2024; Agent 3 followed in September 2025.

latest_major_update.comment: Agent 3: more autonomous testing and fixing loop.

rules.comment: Project context exists inside Replit, but no AGENTS.md-style standard confirmed. Location: hosted/web/API-managed; no local disk folder documented.

skills.comment: No reusable skill-folder system found. Location: hosted/web/API-managed; no local disk folder documented.

hooks.comment: Agent 3 can run autonomous test/fix loops, but not general user-defined lifecycle hooks.

mcp_servers.comment: No MCP configuration found in public Agent docs. Location: hosted/web/API-managed; no local disk folder documented.

custom_commands.comment: Prompt/chat workflow, not user-defined slash-command folders. Location: hosted/web/API-managed; no local disk folder documented.

subagents.comment: Agent 3 can build agents and run background tasks, but no first-class custom subagent framework found.

model_selection.comment: No user model selection for Replit Agent found.

approval_mode.comment: User reviews generated work in Replit; granular command approval controls are not documented.

sandbox_mode.comment: Runs inside Replit-hosted workspaces.

resume.comment: Agent work persists in Replit projects, but no explicit session resume command.

continue.comment: Can continue in the same hosted workspace/project.

non_interactive.comment: No public headless/CLI automation mode found.

output_format.comment: No machine-readable output mode found.

statusline.comment: Hosted UI, no custom CLI statusline.

telemetry.comment: No Agent-specific telemetry toggle found.

hosted_agent.comment: Hosted app-building agent in Replit.

custom_model_provider.comment: No BYO model/provider support found.

pricing.comment: Replit pricing and Agent limits vary by plan.

notes: Best for hosted app prototyping/build-deploy workflows, not for local repo terminal automation.

transcripts.comment: Location: hosted/web/API-managed session history; no local transcript directory documented.

name: Roo Code

form_factor.comment: VS Code extension + CLI. Per-form-factor links: Extension: https://docs.roocode.com/getting-started/installing.

released_in.comment: Forked from Cline. Earliest releases ~mid-2025. Reached v3.54.0 by shutdown on May 15, 2026.

latest_major_update.comment: Final release v3.54.0 on May 15, 2026 (shutdown/sunsetting release). Repository archived, read-only.

rules.comment: Comprehensive: .roorules, .roo/rules/, .clinerules fallback, ~/.roo/rules/ global dir, AGENTS.md/AGENT.md support, mode-specific rules via rules-{modeSlug}. Location: no public disk folder/file location found in docs reviewed.

skills.comment: Full skills (added v3.38.0, Dec 2025). Follows agentskills.io spec: SKILL.md with frontmatter, progressive discovery, mode-specific targeting, bundled resources. Location: no public disk folder/file location found in docs reviewed.

hooks.comment: No lifecycle hooks system found in any documentation. Built-in slash commands exist but no user-defined lifecycle hooks.

mcp_servers.comment: Full MCP since v2.2.0. STDIO, Streamable HTTP, SSE transports. Roo Code Marketplace for one-click MCP install. Per-tool alwaysAllow permissions. Location: no public disk folder/file location found in docs reviewed.

custom_commands.comment: Full slash commands: .roo/commands/ (project) and ~/.roo/commands/ (global). Frontmatter with description, argument-hint, mode targeting. Built-in /init command. Location: no public disk folder/file location found in docs reviewed.

subagents.comment: Full orchestration via Orchestrator Mode (Boomerang Mode). new_task tool delegates to specialized modes. Isolated context with explicit context transfer.

model_selection.comment: 22+ documented providers: Anthropic, OpenAI, AWS Bedrock, DeepSeek, Google Gemini, LM Studio, Ollama, OpenRouter, and more. Sticky models per mode.

approval_mode.comment: Granular auto-approval: read/write operations, approved commands (allowlist/denylist), MCP tools (dual permission), mode switching, subtasks, follow-up questions.

sandbox_mode.comment: No explicit sandbox mode. Checkpoints provide git-based recovery. .rooignore for access control. No isolated/sandboxed execution environment.

resume.comment: Checkpoints provide 'Restore Files & Task'. CLI has --create-with-session-id and session resume/history support (v3.51.0+).

continue.comment: CLI session resume/history support. Per-task history persists and can be resumed. History view shows past tasks for reopening.

non_interactive.comment: CLI binary (roo) with headless mode. NDJSON stdin/stdout stream protocol. Subcommands: run, list, upgrade. Platform binaries available.

output_format.comment: CLI supports NDJSON stdin stream protocol for structured I/O. exitCode in tool_result events. No formal --json or --print flag.

statusline.comment: No custom status line feature found. VS Code extension has chat panel UI but no configurable status line.

telemetry.comment: PostHog-powered. Anonymous feature usage, VS Code machine ID, error reports. No PII/code/prompts. Opt-out available. API error telemetry per provider.

custom_model_provider.comment: OpenAI Compatible provider for arbitrary API endpoints. LiteLLM routing. 22+ built-in providers. Debug proxy support. Model-agnostic.

notes: Roo Code was a popular Cline fork (24k stars, 574k VS Code installs) that was shut down on May 15, 2026. The repository is now archived and read-only. VS Code extension-host API only; no standalone npm agent SDK. Community forks include ZooCode and Cline.

pricing.comment: Free and open-source (Apache 2.0). Users paid their own LLM provider API costs. Roo Code Router was a paid routing service. Now discontinued.

hosted_agent.comment: Runs locally as VS Code extension or CLI. Project is now archived.

transcripts.comment: Location: transcript/session disk storage was not found in public docs reviewed.

name: Windsurf Cascade

form_factor.comment: Standalone VS Code fork IDE + JetBrains plugin + CLI. Per-form-factor links: IDE: https://windsurf.com/download; Extension: https://docs.windsurf.com/windsurf/cascade/cascade-in-ides; CLI: https://docs.windsurf.com/windsurf/cascade/command-line.

released_in.comment: Originally launched as 'Codeium' IDE, rebranded to 'Windsurf' ~November 2024. Windsurf 2.0 shipped April 15, 2026.

latest_major_update.comment: Windsurf 2.0 (April 15, 2026): Agent Command Center (Kanban board), Devin cloud agent integration, Spaces. Latest patch: v2.3.9 (May 17, 2026).

rules.comment: Three scopes: Global (~/.codeium/windsurf/memories/global_rules.md, always-on), Workspace (.windsurf/rules/*.md with frontmatter modes), System (/etc/windsurf/rules/*.md). AGENTS.md support. Location: no public disk folder/file location found in docs reviewed.

skills.comment: Full support. SKILL.md files with YAML frontmatter. Progressive disclosure. Scopes: Workspace .windsurf/skills/, Global ~/.codeium/windsurf/skills/. Discovers .agents/skills/ and .claude/skills/. Location: no public disk folder/file location found in docs reviewed.

hooks.comment: 12 hook events: pre/post read/write code, pre/post run command, pre/post MCP tool use, pre/post user prompt, post cascade response. Pre-hooks can block with exit code 2.

mcp_servers.comment: Full native MCP. MCP Marketplace with one-click install. stdio, Streamable HTTP, SSE transports. OAuth support. Admin whitelists for teams. Location: no public disk folder/file location found in docs reviewed.

custom_commands.comment: Custom commands via Workflows (slash commands /[workflow-name]) and Command palette. Terminal has 4 auto-execution levels. Location: no public disk folder/file location found in docs reviewed.

subagents.comment: Devin Cloud (autonomous agent on own VM), Devin Local Agent, Fast Context (specialized 20x faster retrieval), Agent Command Center manages all agents in Kanban view.

model_selection.comment: Full selection: SWE-1.6/1.5, Claude Opus/Sonnet, GPT-5.x series, Gemini, DeepSeek V4, Kimi, Grok, and more. Adaptive (intelligent router) and Arena Mode (side-by-side).

approval_mode.comment: 4 terminal levels: Disabled (require approval), Allowlist Only, Auto (Cascade judges), Turbo (all auto-executed). Allow/deny lists. Cascade Code/Chat/Ask mode distinctions.

sandbox_mode.comment: Devin cloud agent runs on isolated VM. Git worktrees isolate parallel sessions. Dev Containers support. No standalone sandbox toggle for the IDE.

resume.comment: Cascade has 'continue' button. Auto-Continue setting. Named checkpoints/snapshots for reversion. Sessions persist and switch via dropdown.

continue.comment: Auto-Continue setting automatically continues when hitting tool-call limits. Queued messages for follow-ups while Cascade is working. Real-time editor awareness.

non_interactive.comment: windsurf CLI available in PATH. Devin CLI agent runs in terminal. No documented --non-interactive flag for the IDE.

output_format.comment: Enterprise analytics API available. post_cascade_response_with_transcript hook writes full conversation to JSONL files at ~/.windsurf/transcripts/{trajectory_id}.jsonl.

statusline.comment: No customizable status line like Cursor. Status bar has model indicator and plan/usage info. Agent Command Center is a Kanban dashboard of all agents.

telemetry.comment: Telemetry settings mentioned in troubleshooting docs. Enterprise analytics API. No documented granular telemetry opt-out beyond standard VS Code settings.

custom_model_provider.comment: BYOK for Anthropic models (Claude Sonnet 4, Opus 4) for Free/Pro users. No generic custom OpenAI-compatible endpoint configuration documented.

notes: Windsurf by Codeium (acquired by Cognition AI in April 2026) is a standalone VS Code fork IDE with integrated coding agent (Cascade). Enterprise analytics API only; no agent embed SDK. Competes directly with Cursor IDE.

pricing.comment: Free ($0): Unlimited autocomplete, 50 Cascade requests weekly. Pro ($20/mo): Standard daily usage + extra at API prices. Max ($200/mo): Heavy daily usage. Teams ($40/user/mo).

hosted_agent.comment: Devin cloud agent integration provides managed agent platform.

transcripts.comment: Location: transcript/session disk storage was not found in public docs reviewed.
